In this captivating episode of the Bigfoot Society Podcast, host Jeremiah Byron sits down with Robert, a 48-year-old rock climber with a remarkable history of Bigfoot encounters. Robert moved to Heavener, Oklahoma at the age of 14, where his fascinating journey with the elusive creature began.

From the moment he set foot in Oklahoma, Robert started noticing signs of Bigfoot's presence. During a hog hunt, he stumbled upon massive Bigfoot tracks that left him both intrigued and awestruck. While exploring the Atoka and McGee Creek area for potential rock climbing spots, Robert and his companions embarked on a wooded trail, only to be engulfed by an otherworldly experience. A thunderous whoop pierced the air, followed by a chorus of spine-chilling howls. Their surroundings became an eerie symphony of mysterious sounds, culminating in the astonishing sight of a tree forcefully pushed down in close proximity to their location.

Undeterred by these encounters, Robert sought out new adventures, discovering a nearby area for rock climbing and camping. To his surprise, a Choctaw gentleman informed him that this particular spot was believed to be Sasquatch's dwelling. Eager to further explore the mysteries of McGee Creek, Robert returned with his companions for a primitive camping experience, only to be confronted by an intensely chilling encounter in the dead of night at their campsite.

Ever inquisitive, Robert shares his discoveries with fellow enthusiasts. He recounts his visits to a rock climbing area referred to by locals as "Wood Booger Hollow," where he and his companions unexpectedly stumbled upon colossal 16-inch tracks. Intrigued, Robert made multiple return trips to the area, hoping to uncover more evidence.

In a spine-tingling twist, Robert shares a second-hand account from a hunter who witnessed a brutal attack by a group of Sasquatch on a pack of hogs while perched in his tree stand. The sheer ferocity of the encounter left an indelible impression on the hunter and added another layer of mystery to the ever-elusive creature.

As the episode draws to a close, Robert recounts a harrowing experience while leading a Boy Scout troop along the Buffalo River in North West Arkansas. During a solo hike ahead of the scouts, he finds himself face-to-face with a Class A Sasquatch at an alarmingly close range, an encounter that will forever be etched in his memory.

Tune in to this enthralling episode as Robert delves deep into his extraordinary encounters with Bigfoot, offering a unique perspective on these legendary creatures and the enduring mystery that surrounds them.

~

snippet.title: Lou Simms on RICO Case, 30-Year Sentence as the Leader of the 142nd St. Lynchmob (Full Interview)
snippet.description:
Former Harlem street figure Lou Simms sat down for his first-ever interview with VladTV, where he started off speaking to VladTV correspondent Cavario about growing up in Harlem in the 1960s. Lou went on to speak about getting in the streets at a young age and going to prison in 10th grade, and he also addressed being the former leader of the 142nd St. Lynchmob. Lou also spoke about going to prison for five years and getting out at the height of the crack era, and he also addressed one of his co-defendants cooperating. During the conversation, Lou spoke about being offered plea deals and facing a triple life sentence. In the middle of the interview, Lou was joined by his friend, OG Danny, who shared stories about his time in the streets with Lou. Click the above video to hear the full interview.

Interview by: Cavario

snippet.description:

snippet.title: Wrestling with Sales: Ross and Marshall Von Erich building on a legendary legacy.
snippet.description:
Today on Wrestling with Sales I'm welcoming Ross and Marshall Von Erich, the Third Generation of one of Wrestling's Royal Families the Von Erichs into the Interview Ring to talk about how they dealt with expectations and the pressure of legacy to become Tag Team Champions in @mlw and headline cards across the country. 
We talk about their early career in the @noah_ghcENG  Dojo in Japan and at Harley Race's @WorldLeagueWrestling in Eldon, MO. 
Despite being born into Pro Wrestling, under the guidance of their father @WWE Hall of Famer Kevin Von Erich, they started at the lowest levels and worked their way up. Learning important lessons of discipline and dealing with pressure along the way. 
We get into the Major Motion Picture in production now, the Iron Claw starring Zac Efron and how they feel about their families story reaching the big screen. 
We also get into their contracts coming due with @mlw next month and what the future holds for the Von Erichs. The next generation is here now and the lessons they learned along the way can be used by anyone. Plus we get a word from @Montway to make you job easier at the dealership. 
The elements of success are universal and the lessons learned need to be applied. 
Like and Subscribe to @geraldbent for new episodes every week!

snippet.title: Vivy Mitchell: Attaque contre la résidence du Premier ministre
snippet.description:
Des individus armés ont attaqué en milieu de journée jeudi   la résidence  du Premier ministre Ariel Henry à Vivy Michell

Les concernés se sont présentés comme des policiers, indignés suite à l'assassinat d'au moins six de leurs collègues à Liancourt, dans le département de l'Artibonite.

Des sources de la Primature et au sein de la police ont confirmé que les protestataires sont effectivement membres de la PNH.

Par ce mouvement, les protestataires veulent dénoncer l'exécution sommaire de leurs frères d'armes devenue monnaie courante ces derniers temps.

Cette situation a provoqué un vent de panique sur les lieux.

A noter qu’un peu plus tard dans la journée, Le Premier Ministre a été pris à partie au niveau du salon diplomatique de l’aéroport international pendant des heures avant de pouvoir quitter les lieux sous forte escorte…

snippet.title: KUNG-FU de Denis Laujol | Teaser 2
snippet.description:
Kung-fu
De Denis Laujol et Emilie Guillaume | Du 31 janvier au 18 février 2023 

Emilie Guillaume est comédienne. Mais cela ne lui suffit pas. A la sortie de l’école, il lui reste quelques centaines d’euros qu’elle claque dans un aller simple pour la Chine. Elle s’inscrit à l’école du cirque de Pékin, la seule qui accepte les étrangers. Surtout s’ils sont capables, comme Emilie, de faire un salto arrière sur place.

En Chine, elle perfectionne son kung-fu. Puis devient cascadeuse. Elle pratique plusieurs arts martiaux différents, au rythme de quatre heures par jour. Wushu (Kung-fu moderne à mains nues et avec sabre, nunchaku, lance...), trickz (acrobatie martiale), silat, boxe, catch, wing chun, taekwondo, escrime… Mais son vrai truc, c’est le nunchaku, qu’elle transporte dans son sac à main.

Avec son énergie débordante, ses cheveux en pétard, ses vêtements flashy et son sourire perpétuel, Emilie est régulièrement appelée pour régler des combats sur les scènes de théâtre et les plateaux de cinéma.

Emilie - inconditionnelle de Jackie Chan, de Tarantino et des mangas -, ​son compagnon Felipe Salas, ancien enfant des rues venu de Colombie devenu acrobate et artiste de cirque de renommée internationale ; et le metteur en scène Denis Laujol (Fritland, Pas Pleurer & Le Champ de Bataille), s’appliqueront, avec Kung-fu, ​à combattre de puissants ennemis : le sexisme, la bêtise, la violence, la lourdeur, la déprime...

Et à faire dans le même temps le récit d'un formidable acte d’émancipation féminine d'aujourd'hui.

Attention : ce spectacle comporte de fortes lumières et des effets sonores puissants. Certaines personnes photosensibles ou présentant des sensibilités physiologiques similaires pourraient en être affectées.

localized.title: KUNG-FU de Denis Laujol | Teaser 2
localized.description:
Kung-fu
De Denis Laujol et Emilie Guillaume | Du 31 janvier au 18 février 2023 

Emilie Guillaume est comédienne. Mais cela ne lui suffit pas. A la sortie de l’école, il lui reste quelques centaines d’euros qu’elle claque dans un aller simple pour la Chine. Elle s’inscrit à l’école du cirque de Pékin, la seule qui accepte les étrangers. Surtout s’ils sont capables, comme Emilie, de faire un salto arrière sur place.

En Chine, elle perfectionne son kung-fu. Puis devient cascadeuse. Elle pratique plusieurs arts martiaux différents, au rythme de quatre heures par jour. Wushu (Kung-fu moderne à mains nues et avec sabre, nunchaku, lance...), trickz (acrobatie martiale), silat, boxe, catch, wing chun, taekwondo, escrime… Mais son vrai truc, c’est le nunchaku, qu’elle transporte dans son sac à main.

Avec son énergie débordante, ses cheveux en pétard, ses vêtements flashy et son sourire perpétuel, Emilie est régulièrement appelée pour régler des combats sur les scènes de théâtre et les plateaux de cinéma.

snippet.title: Sexisme chez les enfants : comment lutter contre ? Claire Tran raconte | Speech
snippet.description:
"Le poupon ou la couleur rose du pull de mon fils ; c'est un acte militant, c'est politique"

Les stéréotypes de genre sont imposés dès l'enfance, Claire Tran nous propose quelques pistes pour les contourner.

Elle a cofondé l'association Parents et Féministes, retrouvez son témoignage et 7 autres récits intimes de déclics féministes dans le livre “Mères sans filtre”, aux éditions Solar.

snippet.title: Lady slaps me after getting national record (Clickbait)
snippet.description:
Ecstatic about this to say the least. I've wanted a clock record for around five years ever since almost getting it multiple times, and I wasn't sure if the void I had from not getting a clock record would ever be filled. I first learned 7simul in September and have been practicing it a ton ever since. This is only the 3rd comp I've used it at, and I've already gotten PR (previous comp) and NAR! Came really close to NAR average at a different comp too. I'm suuuuuper hyped all this clock practice paid off. The future of 7 simul is looking very bright, and I will make sure a video tutorial is the next thing I upload. This is only the beginning; the method has so much potential. 

2nd in the world as of January 7th, 2023
Congrats to Vishwa Sankar for a 3rd in the world result of 3.07 as well!

The trailer explores how the real-life Erich wrestling empire was built — and brought to the deadly brink by Fritz Von Erich. Efron and White star as Kevin and Kerry, the oldest and middle Von Erich brothers and the favorites of their father.
